name: Mechanism resolving the H0 tension between early and late universe
closability_note: Many published programs aim at this (early dark energy, modified-recombination via varying alpha or m_e, decaying or interacting dark matter, modified gravity), but no single proposal has been validated. The tension could also resolve experimentally — local-distance-ladder systematics or CMB priors could be wrong. As of 2024 the tension stands at ~5 sigma between SH0ES + Cepheids and Planck + LCDM, with persistent disagreement after a decade of scrutiny. A closing mechanism must either (a) match data on BOTH sides (early and late) without breaking other observables (sum of neutrino masses, S8, BAO), or (b) reveal a systematic in one of the measurements.
summary: No framework in the catalog claims to resolve the H0 tension between CMB-inferred (~67 km/s/Mpc) and late-time-measured (~73 km/s/Mpc) Hubble constant values, currently at ~5 sigma significance.
what_closes_it: A new-physics mechanism producing a ~10% shift in either the sound horizon at last scattering (early-universe route: shorter sound horizon raises inferred H0) or the local distance ladder (late-universe route: less common, harder to evade SN1a self-calibration). Early-route candidates: early dark energy peaking at z ~ 3000 (Karwal-Kamionkowski 2016; Poulin et al. 2019); a varying fine-structure constant or electron mass before recombination shifting the sound horizon (Sekiguchi-Takahashi 2021; Hart-Chluba 2020); a decaying-DM component shifting matter density at decoupling. Late-route candidates: interacting dark sector with momentum exchange (Di Valentino et al.); modified gravity with running of G. The mechanism must respect the BAO + SN1a + S8 + sum-m_nu constraints, which most published proposals address only at the level of mild tension trade-offs.
scale_hint: Mass scale 10^-29 to 10^-27 eV for the early-DE scalar; recombination-era physics at T ~ 0.3 eV. The closing mechanism operates at z ~ 1000-10000, not at the present epoch.

principles_at_stake.note: An early-DE field with V ~ rho_DE at z ~ 3000 has Lambda ~ 0.3 eV decay constant — a new scale unrelated to anything else in the SM, requiring its own naturalness story.

literature_anchors.citation: Karwal and Kamionkowski, 'Dark energy at early times, the Hubble parameter, and the string axiverse', PRD 94, 103523 (2016)
Poulin, Smith, Karwal, Kamionkowski, 'Early dark energy can resolve the Hubble tension', PRL 122, 221301 (2019)
Hart and Chluba, 'Updated fundamental constant constraints from Planck 2018 data and possible relations to the Hubble tension', MNRAS 493, 3255 (2020)
Di Valentino et al., 'In the realm of the Hubble tension — a review of solutions', Class. Quant. Grav. 38, 153001 (2021)
